page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.c4.xlarge generally delivers entry-level performance across most compute and memory workloads. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with uncached read speeds of 22.04 GB/sec and write speeds of 14.84 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in specific single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression at 25.81 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.42 GB/sec, as well as text processing.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but older Intel Xeon architecture, the ecs.c4.xlarge offers a budget-friendly option for compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory throughput or local storage. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded processing, basic text manipulation, and low-traffic web serving. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded compilation, or modern large language model inference due to its constrained memory capacity and lower multi-core efficiency.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.2 GHz on a KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks position the instance primarily in the lower tiers, with weak multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth results. However, it maintains average performance in single-threaded operations like gzip compression and text processing. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, it serves as a cost-effective option for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, and simple text processing workloads that do not demand high memory capacity or modern multi-threaded execution.
